description: Ripe grain and many flowers are 'soriu'. Pale and saturated yellows both keep the word. A source before 'welisha' (color) can narrow the shade when that source matters.
articulatory_notes: Bring the tongue blade close to the alveolar ridge for voiceless 's' and round into mid-back 'o'. Start the stressed second syllable with a tap or short trill for 'r' and high-front 'i'. Keep final rounded high-back 'u' separate from 'i', so the word ends with two distinct vowel beats.
sound_symbolism: The stressed tap and high-front 'i' make the middle syllable the word's sharpest point. Final 'u' stays separate and rounds the sound after it. That bright centre can help recall yellow.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: yellow across pale and saturated shades
semantic_domains.nature: grain at harvest and yellow flowers offer recurring examples
